meaning: adj.不体面的,不适宜的,不恰当的adv.不体面地
examples: It would be unseemly for judges to receive pay increases when others are having to tighten their belts.
The pampering is almost unseemly, some (including the Palestinians) might say.
It feels unseemly to complain about not getting a raise when your neighbor is unemployed.
We can't legislate common sense, but we can reject leaks and unseemly competition for credit.
Soon enough our daily costumes lay in an unseemly ruined pile at our feet.
